up.method: carbon source for modified Wood-Ljungdahl pathway; no rate reported

sec.method: metabolic by-product of hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis; no rate reported

growth_records.cite: Thevasundaram et al. Engineering nonphotosynthetic carbon fixation for production of bioplastics by methanogenic archaea. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2118638119. [PMC9191640]
growth_records.snippet: relatively fast doubling time for methanogens (~2 h)... M. maripaludis uses CO2 and H2 as carbon and electron sources, respectively, producing acetyl-CoA as an intermediate for biosynthesis and methane.
growth_records.notes: Growth rate CALCULATED from the paper's stated approximate doubling time: the paper describes M. maripaludis S2 as having a 'relatively fast doubling time for methanogens (~2 h)'. mu = ln2/td = 0.693/2.0 h = 0.347/h, rounded 0.35/h. This ~2 h value is a general characterization (Introduction), not a per-condition measurement, hence low confidence. Medium McCas is a rich undefined medium; full composition NOT given in this paper (cited to Tumbula & Whitman 1999, ref 37) so medium_composition_stated=false; only nitrogen sources (NH4Cl + Ala) and casamino acids/vitamins are indicated. Carbon source = CO2, electron donor = H2 (no uptake fluxes reported). Methane is the by-product (no secretion rate reported). O2: obligate anaerobe.

growth_records.medium: McNA-Ala (minimal defined medium with L-alanine as sole nitrogen source)

up.method: 10 mM supplied as sole nitrogen source; oxidatively deaminated by Ald to pyruvate + NH4+; ~4% of product carbon labeled from 13C-Ala; no uptake flux reported

sec.method: reported; HPLC-MS/MS; 16.3 +/- 2.7 mg/L, highest of the three media (McCas, McNA, McNA-Ala)

growth_records.cite: Thevasundaram et al. Engineering nonphotosynthetic carbon fixation for production of bioplastics by methanogenic archaea. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2118638119. [PMC9191640]
growth_records.snippet: we found that growth in McNA-Ala medium led to S-3HB titers of 16.3 +/- 2.7 mg/L, the highest titer of the three media conditions... McNA-Ala, minimal defined medium with 10 mM Ala.
growth_records.notes: S-3HB titer 16.3 +/- 2.7 mg/L reported (product TITER, not a specific secretion flux; no biomass or time-resolved data to compute qP in mmol/gDW/h). Medium McNA-Ala = minimal defined medium with 10 mM L-alanine as sole N source; full mineral base composition NOT given here (cited refs), so medium_composition_stated=false and only the stated 10 mM Ala is listed. No growth rate measured for this condition. Carbon from CO2, electrons from H2 (hydrogenotrophic); no substrate uptake fluxes reported. 37 C, static, 7-day production.

growth_records.strain: S2, engineered PhaABC (PhaA C.acetobutylicum + PhaB H.bluephagenesis + PhaC C.necator) + NadMV salvage + Fdh1 (C.boidinii formate dehydrogenase)
growth_records.medium: McNA (minimal defined medium with 10 mM ammonium chloride)

up.method: carbon source via modified Wood-Ljungdahl pathway; CO2 from formate oxidation also re-fixed; no rate reported
alternative energy source; oxidized by heterologous Fdh1 to regenerate NADH + CO2; no uptake flux reported

growth_records.cite: Thevasundaram et al. Engineering nonphotosynthetic carbon fixation for production of bioplastics by methanogenic archaea. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2022). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2118638119. [PMC9191640]
growth_records.snippet: final titers of 158 +/- 6 mg/L (R-3HB) and 171 +/- 4 mg/L, respectively... These yields correspond to 26.3 +/- 1.6% and 24.0 +/- 1.9%, respectively, of dry cell weight.
growth_records.notes: PHB titer 171 +/- 4 mg/L and yield 24.0 +/- 1.9% of dry cell weight reported directly (product-per-biomass yield; Ypx = 0.240 g PHB/gDCW). These are TITER and biomass-fraction yields, not specific fluxes; no substrate uptake or time-resolved biomass data given to compute qP or qS in mmol/gDW/h. Medium McNA = minimal defined + 10 mM NH4Cl (full base composition cited to refs, not given here) so medium_composition_stated=false; supplemented with 50 mM nicotinamide (NadMV salvage) and formate (Fdh1). Optimized growth: 30 C, 250 rpm, H2/CO2 80/20 to 275 kPa daily, 14 d. No per-condition growth rate reported (organism general doubling ~2 h noted only in Introduction). Obligate anaerobe.

growth_records.medium: B3n medium (B3m without PIPES) amended with acetate, yeast extract, cysteine and sulfide

up.method: experimental, Table 3 (measured over 25 h batch on H2/CO2/acetate)

growth_records.cite: Stolyar et al. Metabolic modeling of a mutualistic microbial community. Molecular Systems Biology (2007). DOI:10.1038/msb4100131. [PMC1847946]
growth_records.snippet: Table 3: Metabolite Experimental data — Hydrogen uptake 0.65, Acetate uptake 0.006, Carbon dioxide uptake 0.21, Methane production 0.2 (mmol/h); Biomass production 0.294 mg DCW/h.
growth_records.notes: M. maripaludis S2 monoculture, this paper's own measurement over 25 h batch on H2+CO2+acetate. Table 3 experimental column: H2 uptake 0.65, acetate uptake 0.006, CO2 uptake 0.21, CH4 production 0.2 mmol/h; biomass production 0.294 mg DCW/h (whole-culture/bottle rates, not per gDW). Temperature not stated for this specific monoculture growth experiment (routine culturing was 37 C, 200 rpm); left null. pH 7.2 taken from B3m base medium. Base medium B3n = B3m minus Na2-PIPES; monoculture amended with 10 mM Na acetate, 2 g/L yeast extract, elevated NaCl 4 g/L, 3 mM cysteine, 0.5 g/L sodium sulfide (Methods 'Experimental determination of growth and metabolic activity'). No mu/doubling derivable: only biomass production rate (mg DCW/h) given, standing biomass not stated, so mu=ln2/td cannot be computed. Thauer's vitamins per L stock: biotin 0.02 g, folic acid 0.02 g, pyridoxine HCl 0.1 g, thiamine HCl 0.05 g, riboflavin 0.05 g, nicotinic acid 0.05 g, DL-pantothenate 0.05 g, p-aminobenzoic acid 0.05 g, vitamin B12 0.01 g. Trace minerals per L stock: FeCl2.4H2O 1.0 g, MnCl2.4H2O 0.5 g, CoCl2.4H2O 0.3 g, ZnCl2 0.2 g, Na2MoO4.4H2O 0.05 g, H3BO3 0.02 g, NiSO4.6H2O 0.1 g, CuCl2.2H2O 0.002 g, Na2SeO3.5H2O 0.006 g, Na2WO4.2H2O 0.008 g.

growth_records.medium: Modified coculture medium (CCM) with acetate replacing lactate

up.method: Growth-limiting energy substrate (H2 limitation); specific rate not reported and not derivable (no biomass gDW, no gas balance given for monoculture)
Carbon/energy substrate for hydrogenotrophic methanogenesis; rate not reported
30 mM acetate supplied for carbon assimilation; specific uptake rate not reported

sec.method: Methanogenesis product; specific rate not reported for monoculture

growth_records.cite: Walker et al. Functional responses of methanogenic archaea to syntrophic growth. ISME J (2012). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2012.60. [PMC3475374]
growth_records.snippet: M. maripaludis monocultures were grown in Bioflo 110 bioreactors... on modified CCM containing 30 mM acetate instead of lactate. Sodium sulfide was replaced in the medium with sparging... of a 1% H2S:N2 mixture... All the transcription data
growth_records.notes: H2-limited chemostat monoculture run at the SAME generation time as the coculture (stated), so mu = D = 0.039 h-1 and doubling time = ln2/0.039 = 17.7 h (inherited from the coculture retention time of 25.6 h). Energy substrate = H2 (limiting) + CO2; acetate (30 mM) supplied only for carbon assimilation (M. maripaludis is autotrophic/acetate-assimilating). No biomass dry weight and no monoculture-specific gas balance reported, so no specific uptake/secretion fluxes derivable. CCM composition cited to Walker et al. 2009, not in this paper -> medium_composition_stated=false.

up.units: mL/min (gas supply flow to chemostat, H2-excess; not a biomass-specific rate)

sec.units: not numerically stated in text (rates measured for all cultures; values shown only in Fig. 1)

growth_records.cite: Yoon et al. A systems level predictive model for global gene regulation of methanogenesis in a hydrogenotrophic methanogen. Genome Research (2013). DOI:10.1101/gr.153916.112. [PMC3814884]
growth_records.snippet: The dilution rate was held constant at 0.083 h-1. For time-series array data, cultures before perturbation were allowed to reach steady state... The standard gassing regime was a 110-mL/min H2, 40-mL/min CO2, 35-mL/min Ar, and 15-mL/min H2S
growth_records.notes: At chemostat steady state mu = dilution rate D. D explicitly stated = 0.083 h-1, so mu = 0.083 h-1 (growth_method=calculated via mu=D). Doubling time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.083 = 8.35 h. Steady-state cell density OD660 between 0.6 and 0.8 (text). Medium only partially specified in-text: standard PO4 0.8 mM (limited 0.12 mM WT / 0.18 mM for dMMP1100), NH4+ 10 mM (limited 2.8 mM); full mineral-medium recipe not given (cited). H2/CO2 supplied as gas flow (110 mL/min H2 excess, 21 mL/min limiting; 40 mL/min CO2) — these are supply flows, not biomass-specific uptake rates, so no qS in mmol/gDW/h derivable. CH4 production measured for all cultures but numeric rates are only in Fig. 1 (not in text). Same D=0.083 h-1 chemostat applied to WT MM901 and to deletion strains (dmtd, dfruAdfrcA, dMMP1447, dMMP0719, dMMP1100); growth rate identical (=D) across strains, hence single record. Temperature and pH not stated in text.

growth_records.cite: Lei Yue et al. The conserved ribonuclease aCPSF1 triggers genome-wide transcription termination of Archaea via a 3'-end cleavage mode. Nucleic Acids Research (2020). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaa702. [PMC7515710]
growth_records.snippet: ▽aCPSF1 exhibited markedly reduced growth at 22°C (μ = 0.035 h−1 versus 0.06 h−1 of S2, Figure 1B)
growth_records.notes: Measured specific growth rate μ=0.06 h−1 for wild-type strain S2 at 22°C, read from growth curves (Figure 1B). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/μ = 0.693147/0.06 = 11.55 h. No substrate uptake or product secretion rates were measured in this paper (transcription-termination study). Medium is McF (composition cited from ref 28, not stored in-text) so medium_composition left empty. pH not stated. M. maripaludis is a hydrogenotrophic methanogen (H2/CO2 or formate) but substrate and methane rates were not quantified here. At 37°C S2 had a 'similar growth rate' to the mutant but no numeric μ was given (Supplementary Figure S1C), so no 37°C record was emitted.

growth_records.cite: Lei Yue et al. The conserved ribonuclease aCPSF1 triggers genome-wide transcription termination of Archaea via a 3'-end cleavage mode. Nucleic Acids Research (2020). DOI:10.1093/nar/gkaa702. [PMC7515710]
growth_records.snippet: ▽aCPSF1 exhibited markedly reduced growth at 22°C (μ = 0.035 h−1 versus 0.06 h−1 of S2, Figure 1B)
growth_records.notes: Measured μ=0.035 h−1 for the Mmp-aCPSF1 expression-depleted strain at 22°C (Figure 1B). Doubling time calculated: td = ln2/μ = 0.693147/0.035 = 19.80 h. This is an engineered depletion strain (indigenous Mmp-aCPSF1 knocked out, expression driven by a repressible tetO system; ~3-fold lower aCPSF1 protein than 37°C culture), included because μ was directly measured; note reduced growth is a genetic-perturbation phenotype, not wild-type physiology. A ~2-fold longer bulk mRNA half-life (8.4 min vs 4.7 min for S2) was also reported but is not a growth-physiology rate. No uptake/secretion/MFA fluxes measured.

growth_records.medium: CCM with unlimited H2 (monoculture reference growth rate)
growth_records.cite: Brileya, Kristen A. et al. Biofilm growth mode promotes maximum carrying capacity and community stability during product inhibition syntrophy. Frontiers in Microbiology (2014). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2014.00693. [PMC4266047]
growth_records.snippet: the doubling time of M. maripaludis with unlimited H2 in CCM is 5 h (k = 0.14 h-1)
growth_records.notes: Monoculture specific growth rate stated in text: M. maripaludis with unlimited H2 in CCM, doubling 5 h, k=0.14 /h (mu=0.14 /h; ln2/0.14=4.95 h ~5 h, consistent). Stated as characteristic value; may derive from prior work. No numeric H2 uptake or CH4 secretion rate reported for this specific monoculture condition.

up.method: 200 mM sodium formate supplied as stoichiometric electron donor for CO2 reduction to CH4

up.units: mmol/gDW/h (not quantified; anaplerotic electron donor via Eha)

sec.units: not quantified in batch (product of methanogenesis)
0.16% +/- 0.02% of gas phase at 2 atm (headspace accumulation via F420-dependent formate:H2 lyase)

growth_records.cite: Costa et al. H2-Independent Growth of the Hydrogenotrophic Methanogen Methanococcus maripaludis. mBio (2013). DOI:10.1128/mBio.00062-13. [PMC3585446]
growth_records.method: reported (qualitative growth curves; no numeric mu given)
growth_records.snippet: The wild-type strain grown on formate can accumulate H2 in the culture headspace to a concentration of 0.16% +/- 0.02% of the gas phase at 2 atm pressure (mean +/- standard deviation for three biological replicates)
growth_records.notes: Wild-type reference. Hydrogenotrophic methanogen: reduces CO2 to CH4 using formate or H2 as electron donor. Medium McCas + 200 mM sodium formate + 200 mM MOPS pH 7; H2-CO2 (20:80, 40 psi = 2 atm) headspace. Growth robust but no numeric mu/doubling time (only OD660 curves, Fig 5A). Measured value: headspace H2 = 0.16%+/-0.02% gas phase (formate:H2 lyase equilibrium). Temperature not stated in Methods (cites prior refs). CH4 is the methanogenic product (measured by GC, Fig 5B) but rate not given for WT batch.

growth_records.strain: delta-6H2ase mutant (delta-vhuAU delta-vhcA delta-fruA delta-frcA delta-hmd delta-ehbN)
growth_records.medium: McCas medium with formate + CO (no H2)

up.units: mmol/gDW/h (not quantified; 5% CO oxidized to CO2 via ACS/CODH, anaplerotic only)

sec.units: not quantified (methanogenesis product; growth slower than with H2)

growth_records.cite: Costa et al. H2-Independent Growth of the Hydrogenotrophic Methanogen Methanococcus maripaludis. mBio (2013). DOI:10.1128/mBio.00062-13. [PMC3585446]
growth_records.method: reported (qualitative growth curves; growth much slower with CO than with H2)
growth_records.snippet: CO promoted growth in the absence of H2 ... Growth rates were much lower with CO in place of H2 ... when the CO2-reducing subunits were genetically eliminated (delta-6H2ase-delta-cdh), growth no longer occurred with CO and formate as the on
growth_records.notes: delta-6H2ase mutant grows H2-independently when 5% CO is present with formate; CO oxidation via ACS/CODH generates anaplerotic reduced ferredoxin. Growth qualitative (Fig 1), much slower than formate+H2. 5% CO insufficient as stoichiometric electron donor (functions only anaplerotically). No numeric rates. Deletion of cdh (CO2-reducing subunits) abolishes CO-dependent growth.

growth_records.strain: delta-7H2ase_sup (all 7 hydrogenases deleted + GAPOR-upregulating suppressor; delta-vhuAU delta-vhcA delta-fruA delta-frcA delta-hmd delta-ehbN delta-ehaNO)
growth_records.medium: McCas medium with formate (sole electron donor)

up.units: mmol/gDW/h (not quantified; sole electron donor for all 4 reduction steps of methanogenesis)

sec.units: not quantified (methanogenesis limited by reduced-ferredoxin availability -> slow growth)
incapable of H2 production (lacks formate:H2 lyase)

growth_records.cite: Costa et al. H2-Independent Growth of the Hydrogenotrophic Methanogen Methanococcus maripaludis. mBio (2013). DOI:10.1128/mBio.00062-13. [PMC3585446]
growth_records.method: reported (qualitative; grew to maximum OD660 within 24 h on transfer)
growth_records.snippet: The delta-7H2ase_sup mutant grew in the absence of H2, and H2 did not stimulate growth ... Upon transfer to new medium, each strain tested routinely grew to maximum optical density at 660 nm (OD660) within 24 h
growth_records.notes: delta-7H2ase_sup lacks all hydrogenase activity yet grows on formate alone via GAPOR-driven ferredoxin reduction. Growth qualitative (Figs 2,3); reaches max OD660 within 24 h; slower than WT (reduced-ferredoxin limited). No numeric mu. Incapable of H2 production (no formate:H2 lyase). Note: parent delta-6H2ase does NOT grow on formate alone without the suppressor mutation.

growth_records.strain: delta-7H2ase_sup-frc (delta-7H2ase_sup complemented with F420-reducing hydrogenase Frc on pLW40)

up.units: mmol/gDW/h (not quantified; 380 mM feed, sole electron donor)

sec.units: mmol/gDW/h (after metronidazole ferredoxin oxidation; ~5-fold rise; approaches 5 mmol/gDW/h at higher gas flow)
100-500 µmol/gDW during active growth (per-biomass amount, not per hour); higher at 230 vs 25 mL/min gas flow

growth_records.cite: Costa et al. H2-Independent Growth of the Hydrogenotrophic Methanogen Methanococcus maripaludis. mBio (2013). DOI:10.1128/mBio.00062-13. [PMC3585446]
growth_records.method: reported (continuous culture near washout; numeric mu not given; medium flow 0.125 L/h > growth, alt 0.031 L/h)
growth_records.snippet: the delta-7H2ase_sup-frc mutant produced between 100 and 500 µmol H2 gdw-1 ... after 1 h, H2 production was found to have increased 5-fold to ~2.5 mmol gdw-1 h-1. With increased gas flow, this rate approached 5 mmol gdw-1 h-1
growth_records.notes: delta-7H2ase_sup-frc: only measured PER-BIOMASS-PER-TIME rate in paper = H2 production ~2.5 mmol gdw-1 h-1 (up to ~5 with higher gas flow) AFTER metronidazole addition (oxidizes ferredoxin, perturbed condition). During normal active growth: 100-500 µmol H2 gdw-1 (per-biomass amount, no time base given). Batch culture same strain: headspace H2 up to 2.32%+/-0.79% gas phase. Chemostat: 380 mM formate, 0.2% Casamino Acids, ampicillin 100 µg/mL, Ar-CO2-1%H2S sparge, pH 6.95, 50 rpm; 'dilution rate' reported as volumetric flow 0.125 L/h (also 0.031 L/h) NOT per-h D (working volume not given), so mu=D not computable. Temperature not stated.

growth_records.medium: Simplified McN / JD high-salt minimal medium (anoxic)

up.method: derived from stoichiometry 4 H2 + CO2 -> CH4 + 2 H2O: 4 x 40.8 mmol CH4/gDW/h
derived: CO2 consumed ~= CH4 produced (1:1) = 40.8 mmol CH4/gDW/h; biomass carbon negligible (Y=1.41 gDW/mol CH4)

sec.method: measured specific methanogenesis rate at chemostat steady state (GC of gas phase); reported 40.8 +/- 10.2

product_yields.note: 1.41 +/- 0.22 g DW per mol CH4 (biomass yield on methanogenesis)

growth_records.cite: Xue, Jie et al. Effects of CO2 and H2 limitations on Methanococcus maripaludis. Microbiology Spectrum (2025). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.00359-25. [PMC12403587]
growth_records.snippet: Biomass yields were 1.41 +/- 0.22 g DW mol-1 CH4 vs 1.49 +/- 0.27 g DW mol-1 CH4 for H2 and CO2 as the limiting substrates, respectively, while specific methanogenesis rates were 40.8 +/- 10.2 mmol CH4 h-1 g DW-1 and 44.1 +/- 15.6 mmol CH4
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, H2-limited steady state. MEASURED: specific methanogenesis (CH4 secretion) = 40.8 +/- 10.2 mmol CH4/gDW/h; biomass yield = 1.41 +/- 0.22 gDW/mol CH4. Dilution rate (=steady-state mu) not stated numerically in main text (points to supplemental figs). H2/CO2 uptake rates DERIVED from methanogenesis stoichiometry 4 H2 + CO2 -> CH4 + 2 H2O: H2 = 4 x 40.8 = 163.2; CO2 = 40.8 mmol/gDW/h (flagged non-experimental). OD-to-DW coefficient 0.34 gDW/OD/L used. Medium prepared anoxically at pH 7.0 +/- 0.2; either JD or simplified McN high-salt minimal medium (McN composition listed).

growth_records.medium: Simplified McN / JD high-salt minimal medium (anoxic)

up.method: derived from stoichiometry 4 H2 + CO2 -> CH4 + 2 H2O: 4 x 44.1 mmol CH4/gDW/h
derived: CO2 consumed ~= CH4 produced (1:1) = 44.1 mmol CH4/gDW/h

sec.method: measured specific methanogenesis rate at chemostat steady state (GC); reported 44.1 +/- 15.6

growth_records.cite: Xue, Jie et al. Effects of CO2 and H2 limitations on Methanococcus maripaludis. Microbiology Spectrum (2025). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.00359-25. [PMC12403587]
growth_records.snippet: specific methanogenesis rates were 40.8 +/- 10.2 mmol CH4 h-1 g DW-1 and 44.1 +/- 15.6 mmol CH4 h-1 g DW-1 ... indicating no significant difference in biomass yield and methanogenesis rate between the two conditions
growth_records.notes: Chemostat, CO2-limited steady state. MEASURED: CH4 secretion = 44.1 +/- 15.6 mmol CH4/gDW/h; biomass yield = 1.49 +/- 0.27 gDW/mol CH4. Authors report no significant difference vs H2-limitation. Dilution rate not stated numerically. H2/CO2 uptake DERIVED from 4 H2 + CO2 -> CH4 + 2 H2O: H2 = 4 x 44.1 = 176.4; CO2 = 44.1 mmol/gDW/h (non-experimental).

growth_records.medium: JD high-salt minimal medium (CO2/bicarbonate-free, closed batch)

up.method: ODE fit to time-series DIC decrease + CH4 formation; half-saturation constant for DIC (electron acceptor) at pH 7, 30C

sec.units: Vmax not reported as absolute rate; near-max above ~350 Pa pCO2
sec.method: GC gas-phase CH4; Michaelis-Menten Vmax/Km analysis

growth_records.cite: Xue, Jie et al. Effects of CO2 and H2 limitations on Methanococcus maripaludis. Microbiology Spectrum (2025). DOI:10.1128/spectrum.00359-25. [PMC12403587]
growth_records.snippet: The apparent Km for DIC of M. maripaludis ... was determined to be 0.60 (95% CI: 0.48-3.83) mmol L-1 ... mu_max was estimated at 0.16 (95% CI: 0.08, 0.49) h-1 for M. maripaludis
growth_records.notes: Batch kinetics record. MEASURED: apparent Km(DIC/CO2) = 0.60 mmol/L (95% CI 0.48-3.83); mu_max = 0.16 h-1 (95% CI 0.08-0.49) from batch growth fitting. Doubling time CALCULATED: td = ln2/mu = 0.693/0.16 = 4.33 h. Absolute Vmax CH4 rate not tabulated (kinetics expressed via V/Vmax = S/(Km+S)); 50% Vmax when pCO2 > ~350 Pa at 30C, pH 7. Cultures grown on 4:1 H2:CO2, JD medium, anoxic, pH 7.0. OD range at experiments 0.21-0.47.

up.units: cumulative % conversion to CH4 (per-biomass rate not reported)

sec.units: cumulative % conversion of H2/CO2 (lowest of all SB groups)

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Shaking at 100 rpm (100/sha) resulted in the lowest growth and substrate consumption from all tested groups. Table 1: SB Shaker 100 ... muL 0.07 GTL 26.93 (max OD578 0.9292 at 301 h).
growth_records.notes: Closed-batch 0.05 L serum bottle, 100 rpm shaking (group 100/sha). muL (linear specific growth rate) 0.07 /h and GTL 26.93 h are reported values (Table 1, Formulas 1 and 3: muL=(OD_x/OD_x-1)/dt; GTL=2/muL). Max OD578 0.9292 at 301 h. Substrate (H2/CO2) consumption measured by pressure as % conversion to CH4 in Figure 1B; no per-gDW uptake/secretion rate tabulated, so numeric rates left null. Anaerobic hydrogenotrophic methanogen: 4 H2 + CO2 -> CH4 + 2 H2O.

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Transferring this culture further (100/sha.2) and cultivation under the same conditions showed adaptation and improved GTL (shaker.2, Table 1). SB Shaker.2 100 ... muL 0.25 GTL 7.87 (max OD 1.1929 at 325 h).
growth_records.notes: Same 100 rpm shaking after inoculum transfer (adapted, group shaker.2). muL 0.25 /h, GTL 7.87 h reported (Table 1). Max OD578 1.1929 at 325 h. Consistency check ln2/0.25=2.77 h differs from GTL 7.87 h because the paper uses linear GTL=2/muL (2/0.25=8.0~7.87), not exponential.

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Agitating at 100 rpm by stirring (100/str) was more efficient than shaking at the same speed. SB Stirrer 100 ... muL 0.29 GTL 6.8 (max OD 1.1089 at 325 h).
growth_records.notes: 0.05 L SB, 100 rpm stirring. muL 0.29 /h, GTL 6.8 h (Table 1, linear model GTL=2/muL). Max OD578 1.1089 at 325 h.

up.units: highest cumulative % conversion to CH4 of all SB groups (per-biomass rate not tabulated)

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Stirring at 500 rpm (500/str) ... reached the highest substrate consumption of all tested groups. SB Stirrer 500 ... muL 0.95 GTL 2.11 (max OD 1.2339 at 323 h).
growth_records.notes: 0.05 L SB, 500 rpm stirring. muL 0.95 /h and GTL 2.11 h reported (Table 1, linear model GTL=2/muL=2/0.95=2.11). Max OD578 1.2339 at 323 h. Highest substrate (H2/CO2) consumption of the SB series.

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: The best growth in SB was observed upon agitation at 500 rpm by stirring and feeding two times a day (500/str/2x) where the highest maximum OD578 of 1.29 was reached around 6 days earlier. Table 1: muL 0.94 GTL 2.12 (max OD 1.2924 at 179 h)
growth_records.notes: 0.05 L SB, 500 rpm stirring + double daily H2/CO2 feeding (500/str/2x). muL 0.94 /h, GTL 2.12 h (Table 1). Highest max OD578 1.2924 reached earliest (179 h). Best SB condition.

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Shaken cultures at 100 (100/sha) ... reached the highest OD578 [of SCB]. Groups ... 100/sha and 500/str around 60% [substrate conversion]. Table 1: SCB Shaker 100 muL 0.21 GTL 9.65 (max OD 1.0819 at 325 h).
growth_records.notes: 0.4 L SCB, 100 rpm shaking. muL 0.21 /h, GTL 9.65 h (Table 1). Max OD578 1.0819 at 325 h. Text: 100/sha clustered ~60% H2/CO2->CH4 conversion (approximate, from Figure 2B description).

up.units: approx cumulative % conversion to CH4 (180/sha ~a bit below 90%)

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: the 180/sha group delivered the lowest GTL of 3.06 h. Groups ... 180/sha and 800/str a bit below 90% [conversion]. Table 1: SCB Shaker 180 muL 0.65 GTL 3.06 (max OD 1.0530 at 209 h).
growth_records.notes: 0.4 L SCB, 180 rpm shaking. muL 0.65 /h, GTL 3.06 h (Table 1, lowest generation time of SCB series). Max OD578 1.0530 at 209 h. ~<90% H2/CO2->CH4 conversion (approx, Figure 2B).

up.units: approx cumulative % conversion to CH4 (100/sha/2x below 50%)

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Subjecting 100/sha cultures to double feeding (100/sha/2x) substantially improved growth ... decreased the GTL more than double. Groups 100/str and 100/sha/2x clustered below 50%. Table 1: SCB Shaker/2x 100 muL 0.48 GTL 4.19 (max OD 1.0561
growth_records.notes: 0.4 L SCB, 100 rpm shaking + double daily H2/CO2 feeding. muL 0.48 /h, GTL 4.19 h (Table 1). Max OD578 1.0561 at 291 h. H2/CO2->CH4 conversion clustered below 50% (Figure 2B).

up.units: approx cumulative % conversion to CH4 (100/str below 50%)

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Stirred cultures generally delivered the slowest growth curves ... 100 (100/str) ... remained at the bottom. Table 1: SCB Stirrer 100 muL 0.12 GTL 16.63 (max OD 0.4766 at 326 h).
growth_records.notes: 0.4 L SCB, 100 rpm stirring. muL 0.12 /h, GTL 16.63 h (Table 1, slowest). Max OD578 0.4766 at 326 h. ~<50% conversion.

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: SCB Stirrer 500 muL 0.27 GTL 7.37 (max OD 0.9322 at 321 h). ... 100/sha and 500/str around 60% [conversion].
growth_records.notes: 0.4 L SCB, 500 rpm stirring. muL 0.27 /h, GTL 7.37 h (Table 1). Max OD578 0.9322 at 321 h. ~60% H2/CO2->CH4 conversion.

up.units: approx cumulative % conversion to CH4 (~a bit below 90%)

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: cultures which were stirred at 800 rpm (800/str) showed strong improvement of growth ... delivered their maximum OD578 in the shortest measured time of 158 h. Table 1: SCB Stirrer 800 muL 0.24 GTL 8.48 (max OD 0.9318).
growth_records.notes: 0.4 L SCB, 800 rpm stirring. muL 0.24 /h, GTL 8.48 h (Table 1). Max OD578 0.9318 reached in shortest time (158 h). ~<90% conversion. This SCB/800rpm condition later used as pre-culture for the 15 L scale-up.

up.units: variable low early then high in late exponential (cumulative % conversion)

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: SCB Stirrer 1,100 muL 0.15 GTL 13.74 (max OD 0.6804 at 209 h). Samples from the group 1,100/str showed variating low substrate consumption during the first half ... but reached reproducibly high values during late exponential growt
growth_records.notes: 0.4 L SCB, 1100 rpm stirring. muL 0.15 /h, GTL 13.74 h (Table 1). Max OD578 0.6804 at 209 h. Substrate conversion variable (low early, high late-exponential).

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: stirring at maximum possible 1,400 rpm was tested. Quadruplicate cultures reached a mean maximum OD578 of 0.169 after 96 h. Table 1: SCB Stirrer 1,400 max OD 0.1691 at 96 h; muL and GTL '-' (not determinable).
growth_records.notes: 0.4 L SCB, 1400 rpm stirring (max agitation). Growth strongly inhibited: mean max OD578 only 0.1691 at 96 h; muL/GTL not determinable (Table 1 dashes), so growth_rate/doubling_time null. Included to document shear-inhibited condition.

up.units: sparged feed gas (per-biomass uptake not tabulated)

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: the best run in this study was generated during phase 7 ... highest recorded OD578 for M. maripaludis of 3.38 ... also the highest recorded values for mu and GT of ~0.16 h-1 and ~4.3 h. Table 3: 7.33.1 Step-cons.-OD OD578 3.086 mu 0.16 GT 4
growth_records.notes: 1.5 L fed-batch bioreactor, phase-7 stepwise-conservative ramp with exponential-phase / low-OD inoculum (reactor ID 7.33.1). Exponential mu 0.16 /h and GT 4.44 h reported (Table 3, Formulas 2 and 4: mu=ln(OD_x/OD_x-1)/dt; GT=ln2/mu; ln2/0.16=4.33~4.44). Max OD578 3.086. Highest specific growth rate of the study (~0.16 /h). Sister replicates 4.27.4 (mu 0.16, GT 4.30) and 2.21.4 similar.

growth_records.cite: Palabikyan H et al. Scale-up of biomass production by Methanococcus maripaludis. Frontiers in Microbiology (2022). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2022.1031131. [PMC9727139]
growth_records.snippet: all 3 replicates from phases 1 and 2 aligned ... delivered high OD578 peaks over 2.5. Table 3: 2.21.3 Step-cons OD578 3.3884 mu 0.06 GT 11.20 wet biomass 3.52 g/L (max OD reached ~114 h).
growth_records.notes: 1.5 L fed-batch bioreactor, stepwise-conservative ramp, stationary inoculum (reactor ID 2.21.3). Highest OD578 3.3884 among 1.5 L runs but low exponential mu 0.06 /h, GT 11.20 h (Table 3; ln2/0.06=11.55~11.20). Wet biomass 3.52 g/L. Represents a high-density but slow-mu condition (distinct from the fast phase-7 run).

growth_records.strain: S2 (NCBI taxonomy ID 267377; model basis iMR539); experimental values from Muller et al.
growth_records.medium: Formate medium, batch (composition not stated in this paper)

up.method: catabolic substrate; measured formate uptake flux shown only in figures of cited source (Muller et al.), numeric value not in this paper's text

sec.method: methanogenesis product via Wood-Ljungdahl pathway; measured methane fluxes plotted in Fig 2c/3a (from Muller et al./Richards et al.) but numeric values not tabulated in text

yields.note: maintenance ATP yield reported as 0.5 mol ATP per mol methane (Muller et al.); no measured biomass yield at max growth rate reported

growth_records.cite: Kasem, Ghada S. et al. Whole-cell modeling predicts alternative proteome allocation strategies in the archaeon Methanococcus maripaludis. Scientific Reports (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41598-026-37887-z. [PMC12923902]
growth_records.snippet: The model predicted a specific maximum growth rate of 0.23 per hour under the formate batch condition (Fig. 5a), consistent with the measured data.
growth_records.notes: EXPERIMENTAL VALUE IS CITED, not generated here: measured maximum specific growth rate ~0.23/h on formate batch from Muller et al. (ref 12; the paper's cited proteomics/physiology dataset). doubling_time = ln2/0.23 = 0.6931/0.23 = 3.01 h (calculated). Anaerobic hydrogenotrophic methanogen; formate -> methane. Numeric formate uptake and methane secretion fluxes exist in the source figures but are not stated numerically in this modelling paper, hence rate=null. Model-predicted values (not experimental) at other conditions: CO2+H2 batch max mu 0.32/h and yield 3.98 gDW/mol methane are PREDICTIONS and were excluded.

growth_records.strain: S2 (NCBI taxonomy ID 267377); experimental data from Muller et al. (formate-limited chemostat)
growth_records.medium: Formate-limited defined medium, chemostat (composition not stated in this paper)

up.method: growth-limiting catabolic substrate; measured formate uptake flux in cited-source figures (Muller et al.), numeric not in text

sec.method: methanogenesis product; measured methane flux (mmol gDW-1 h-1) plotted in Fig 2c from Muller et al.; numeric not tabulated in text

yields.note: low measured growth yield at low mu attributed by Muller et al. to cell lysis + maintenance (ATP yield 0.5 mol ATP/mol methane); measured protein mass 0.49-0.76 g/gDW and RNA 0.07-0.11 g/gDW over mu 0.002-0.01 /h

growth_records.cite: Kasem, Ghada S. et al. Whole-cell modeling predicts alternative proteome allocation strategies in the archaeon Methanococcus maripaludis. Scientific Reports (2026). DOI:10.1038/s41598-026-37887-z. [PMC12923902]
growth_records.snippet: under formate-limited conditions, at low growth rates (between 0.002 and 0.01 per hour), the measured protein mass was between 0.49 and 0.76 g protein per gDW, and the RNA mass was between 0.07 and 0.11 g RNA per gDW.
growth_records.notes: EXPERIMENTAL DATA CITED from Muller et al. (ref 12), not generated in this paper. Formate-limited chemostat spanning dilution rates 0.002-0.1 /h; at steady state mu=D. This is a SERIES of steady states, not a single condition - represented here as one aggregate record. growth_rate_per_h set to 0.006 (midpoint of the explicitly-described low-mu window 0.002-0.01 where protein/RNA masses are quoted); doubling_time = ln2/0.006 = 115.5 h (calculated for that midpoint). Uptake/secretion flux values are in source figures only (not numeric in text). Measured biomass composition (protein/RNA per gDW) captured in yields.note. Phosphate-limited chemostat (Gu et al., ref 26) also validated but no distinct in-text numeric rate; not emitted separately.

up.units: H2-limiting substrate; specific uptake rate not tabulated in text (gas feed 10-20 ml/min H2)

sec.units: methane evolution rate measured for 9 steady-state points (bubble flow meter + GC-FID); numeric values in Fig 4, not in text

yields.Yx_gDW_per_mol_CH4: measured across 9 steady states (values in Fig 4); OD660 of 1 = 0.462±0.015 mg DW/ml

growth_records.cite: Richards MA et al. Exploring Hydrogenotrophic Methanogenesis: a Genome Scale Metabolic Reconstruction of Methanococcus maripaludis. Journal of Bacteriology (2016). DOI:10.1128/JB.00571-16. [PMC5116941]
growth_records.snippet: We altered our growth rate of M. maripaludis by varying pump speeds to achieve dilution rates of approximately 0.045 to 0.090 h−1 ... determined growth yields and growth rates (equal to dilution rates) and compared them to measured methane
growth_records.notes: GEM paper (iMR539) with the authors' OWN experimental chemostat data. Wild-type M. maripaludis S2 grown in McNA defined medium (composition in supplement, not in main text → composition:[]). H2-limited chemostat, anaerobic; 9 independent steady states with dilution rate 0.045-0.090 h⁻¹; at steady state growth_rate = dilution rate. Record uses lower-bound D=0.045 h⁻¹ (range 0.045-0.090); doubling_time = ln2/0.045 = 15.4 h (at D=0.090, td=7.7 h). Authors' own td formula: td = ln(2)×(dilution rate×60)⁻¹ (min). Methane evolution rate and growth yield (gDW/mol CH4) measured per point but numeric values only in Fig 4 (not text-extractable). OD660 1 = 0.462±0.015 mg DW/ml. Fitted GAM=169.9 mmol ATP/g cell, NGAM=5.0 mmol ATP/g/h (model params, not physiology). NOTE: intro cites batch doubling time of ~2 h (μ≈0.347 h⁻¹) from ref 12 (Jones et al., original species description) — CITED, not measured here. Temperature/pH not stated in text.

up.method: headspace gas balance via process simulation (Hysys), early exponential phase; 132.13±15.13
headspace gas balance, early exponential phase; 423.06±44.94

sec.method: headspace gas balance (methane evolution), early exponential phase; 105.61±17.75

mfa.note: measured extracellular flux; matched in silico by genome-scale model iMM518 at NGAM=7.836 mmol/gDCW/h, GAM=27.14 mmol/gDCW
measured extracellular flux; H2/CO2 ratio relation vH2/vCO2=2.858+64.759/vCO2 (Eq.1)
measured extracellular flux; CO2-to-CH4 conversion yield 70-95%

growth_records.cite: Goyal, Nishu et al. Flux measurements and maintenance energy for carbon dioxide utilization by Methanococcus maripaludis. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0336-z. [PMC4573941]
growth_records.snippet: The maximum growth rate was estimated to be 0.50 ± 0.05/h for a CO2 uptake of 132.13 ± 15.13 mmol/gDCW/h, H2 uptake of 423.06 ± 44.94 mmol/gDCW/h, and CH4 production of 105.61 ± 17.75 mmol/gDCW/h.
growth_records.notes: First experimental report of CO2/H2 uptake and CH4 production for M. maripaludis on H2/CO2 minimal medium. Early exponential phase: µ_max=0.50±0.05 h-1; doubling_time calculated = ln2/0.50 = 1.386 h (paper states overall doubling ~2 h, which corresponds to the representative µ=0.346 h-1 given in Table 1). Fluxes measured via headspace gas mass balance + Hysys process simulation. Maintenance parameters NGAM=7.836 mmol/gDCW/h and GAM=27.14 mmol/gDCW are MODEL-derived (iMM518 fit to the measured fluxes), reported in yields for completeness. Growth yield 3.549±0.149 gDCW/mol CH4 from DCW-vs-CH4 plot.

up.method: headspace gas balance, late exponential phase (end of 7 h); 107.5±44.94

sec.method: methane evolution rate (MER), late exponential phase; 27.19±17.75

mfa.note: MER close to Lupa et al. cited range 9.40-27.55 mmol/gDCW/h for µ 0.04-0.13/h

growth_records.cite: Goyal, Nishu et al. Flux measurements and maintenance energy for carbon dioxide utilization by Methanococcus maripaludis. Microbial Cell Factories (2015). DOI:10.1186/s12934-015-0336-z. [PMC4573941]
growth_records.snippet: Our observed H2 uptake was 423.06 ± 44.94 mmol/gDCW/h in the early exponential phase, and 107.5 ± 44.94 mmol/gDCW/h at the end of 7 h ... close to our MER of 27.19 ± 17.75 mmol/gDCW/h for a growth rate of 0.064 ± 0.049/h in the late exponen
growth_records.notes: Same batch experiment, LATE exponential phase (rates decline as headspace H2/CO2 is depleted, pressure 250->100 kPa). µ=0.064±0.049 h-1; doubling_time calculated = ln2/0.064 = 10.83 h. CO2 uptake for the late phase not explicitly stated in text (only H2 uptake and CH4 MER given), so omitted rather than invented. MER 27.19 mmol/gDCW/h compared favourably to cited Lupa et al. range 9.40-27.55 mmol/gDCW/h.
